general.prompt: What did Iran claim to have hit in its latest retaliation against the U.S.?
options: The White House lawn with a water balloon
U.S. military facilities in Greenland
More than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait
A flock of pigeons near the Pentagon
general.summary: Iran said it targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait after U.S. strikes on commercial ships.

general.prompt: Why did Denmark's PM vow to defend Greenland?
options: Greenland's polar bears unionized and demanded better fishing rights
Trump revived push for U.S. control of Greenland
Greenland threatened to invade Denmark with ice cubes
Iran launched strikes on Greenland's commercial ships
general.summary: Denmark's prime minister pledged to defend Greenland after President Trump revived his push for U.S. control over the island.

general.prompt: What new privacy-baiting gadget is Meta testing?
options: A hat that reads your thoughts and tweets them
AI glasses that can capture every moment
Socks that track your footsteps and play music
Super sensing glasses that can see through Greenland's ice
general.summary: Meta is testing ‘super sensing’ AI glasses that can capture every moment, sparking a new privacy fight.

general.prompt: Who will Switzerland face in the World Cup quarterfinal?
options: The Moon national team
Denmark's Greenlandic football club
Argentina
A team of AI referees
general.summary: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to set up a quarterfinal match against Argentina.

general.prompt: What is the horoscope for July 8, 2026, most likely about?
options: Tips on how to avoid alien abduction
Predictions on Meta's stock price after the glasses launch
Astrological predictions for each zodiac sign
A recipe for flying spaghetti
general.summary: The horoscope offers astrological predictions for the day, tailored to zodiac signs.

tech.prompt: Meta's new 'super sensing' glasses can record everything you see. What's the main concern?
options: Nintendo is suing over the design
They might make you look like a robot
They run out of battery in 10 minutes
Privacy fight over who gets recorded
tech.summary: Meta is testing AI glasses that can capture moments, raising privacy issues about who gets recorded.

tech.prompt: Nintendo just added four more games to Switch Online's retro library. Which game is among them?
options: Wario Land
Tetris: The Lost Levels
Super Mario 64 but with a dancing Luigi
Minecraft: Game Boy Edition
tech.summary: Nintendo added Wario Land and three other titles to the Game Boy and GBA library.

tech.prompt: Samsung is offering a whopping $1,230 off the Galaxy Z Fold 8. What's the catch?
options: You have to trade in a toaster
You must also buy Meta's AI glasses
It's only available in London during Unpacked
You need to qualify for the deal
tech.summary: Samsung announced a deal for the Galaxy Z Fold 8 ahead of July Unpacked, with a $1,230 discount for qualifying customers.

tech.prompt: Minecraft players can finally do what in the latest update?
options: Use Game Boy emulator inside Minecraft
Fly to the moon
Sit down
Pet every animal in the game
tech.summary: Minecraft's new update lets players sit down without using mods.

tech.prompt: Fans threatening to cancel PS Plus are finding what surprising benefit?
options: Saving money by threatening to quit
Sony sends them a free PS6 prototype
Nintendo gives them a free Switch
You get a lifetime supply of digital games
tech.summary: Sony offered a 50% off coupon to fans who threatened to cancel PS Plus, saving money.

business.prompt: Why is Congress suddenly sweating over AI? Because they're probing Chinese models making their way into U.S. companies! What's the big concern?
options: Potential security and ethical risks from Chinese AI
They want to replace lawmakers with AI
Japanese borrowing costs hit 30-year high due to AI
Chinese AI models are too good at writing haikus
business.summary: A House Committee is investigating risks from U.S. companies using Chinese-made AI models.

business.prompt: Oil prices just did a U-turn faster than a Tesla on Autopilot! Why did Brent crude spike above $76 a barrel?
options: Rivian's stock sale triggered the surge
Everyone suddenly decided to drive gas-guzzlers again
OPEC members started a dance-off
US strikes on Iran escalated tensions
business.summary: Oil prices surged after U.S. strikes on Iran amid renewed violence near the Strait of Hormuz.

business.prompt: What's worse than finding a hair in your food? Finding sharp metal and plastic in your dog's dinner! Which popular pet food is being recalled?
options: A brand that promised unlimited belly rubs
A Chinese AI model used in pet food
Pedigree dog food
Cat food that makes dogs smarter
business.summary: Mars Petcare recalled two lots of Pedigree dog food due to metal and plastic contamination after fraudulent diversion.

business.prompt: Japan's borrowing costs just hit a 30-year high! Why are bond yields soaring like a sumo wrestler on a trampoline?
options: Godzilla took out a huge mortgage
Everyone started buying sushi bonds instead
Oil prices surged due to US strikes
Debt fears over long-term spending plans
business.summary: Japanese government bond yields hit their highest since 1996 due to worries over long-term spending plans.

business.prompt: Rivian's stock just took a nosedive worse than a failed EV prototype! What caused this 18% plunge?
options: Lawmakers probed their use of Chinese AI
A stock sale that worried investors about cash troubles
CEO announced they'd only make flying cars from now on
They painted the cars in invisible ink
business.summary: Rivian's stock sank 18% after a stock sale sparked concerns about the company's cash troubles.

sports.prompt: Which team did Switzerland beat on penalties to set up a World Cup quarterfinal against Argentina?
options: Egypt
Argentina
Colombia
The Swiss National Bank
sports.summary: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties in the World Cup last 16.

sports.prompt: Shohei Ohtani just hit his 300th home run! How fast is he compared to MLB history?
options: Slowest ever (but still impressive!)
5th fastest in MLB history
Exactly average
Fastest ever
sports.summary: Shohei Ohtani hit his 300th home run, becoming the 5th fastest to reach that milestone.

sports.prompt: Who did the Grizzlies land after the Warriors passed on matching an offer sheet?
options: Quinten Post
Shohei Ohtani (he's versatile)
A new post office branch in Memphis
Khris Middleton
sports.summary: The Grizzlies acquired Quinten Post after the Warriors did not match his three-year, $30 million offer sheet.

sports.prompt: Which VAR call raised questions of legitimacy at the World Cup, with Argentina beating which team?
options: The referees' union
Colombia
Egypt
Switzerland
sports.summary: A VAR call in Argentina's win over Egypt raised questions of political influence and fairness.

sports.prompt: Which player is reportedly rejoining the Wizards via a 6-team sign-and-trade deal?
options: A wizard from Hogwarts
Khris Middleton
Quinten Post (he's tall)
Shohei Ohtani (he does everything)
sports.summary: Khris Middleton is reportedly returning to the Washington Wizards in a six-team sign-and-trade.

science.prompt: Which sleepy spacecraft just woke up from a year-long nap and is ready to spill the tea from the Kuiper Belt?
options: Voyager 1
James Webb Space Telescope
New Horizons
The Hubble Space Telescope
science.summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft emerged from its longest hibernation of nearly a year in good health, ready to transmit science data from the distant Kuiper Belt.

science.prompt: What did the James Webb Space Telescope snap for its 4th birthday that looks like a cosmic car crash?
options: A galaxy crash site
A selfie with Pluto
A new planet made of cake
A super-rare soft tissue fossil
science.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope celebrated its 4th birthday with a stunning image of a galaxy crash site.

science.prompt: What super-rare 450-million-year-old fossil did scientists find that isn't just a hard skeleton?
options: A crinoid with soft tissues
A fossilized alien phone
A galaxy crash site
A dinosaur feather
science.summary: Scientists found only the second known crinoid fossil with soft tissues intact, dating back 450 million years.

science.prompt: How big is our Milky Way galaxy, according to new research that used cosmic explosions as rulers?
options: About to collide with Andromeda
Shaped like a doughnut
Exactly the same size
Larger than we thought
science.summary: Astronomers used cosmic explosions to find that the Milky Way galaxy might be larger than previously thought.

science.prompt: What can a new chip do with light beams faster than you can blink—like, a trillionth of a second fast?
options: Redirect light beams
Take a selfie
Wake from hibernation
Make toast
science.summary: A new chip redirects light beams in less than a trillionth of a second, advancing photonic technologies for faster communications and computing.
